prompt: Ultrasound is defined as sound with frequency:

options.text: Exactly equal to audible midrange always

explanation: Diagnostic ultrasound commonly uses MHz frequencies.

options.text: Frequency times period only as wavelength

explanation: λ = c/f; higher frequency → shorter wavelength → better axial resolution generally.

prompt: Propagation speed is determined primarily by:
explanation: Soft tissue average ~1540 m/s; stiffer media generally faster sound.
Reflection depends on impedance mismatch at boundaries.

options.text: Determined only by the medium, not the source

explanation: Source (transducer) determines frequency/period; medium determines speed.
Intensity also ∝ A^2; important for bioeffects/output discussions.

prompt: Attenuation in soft tissue increases when:

options.text: Frequency decreases with path fixed always reducing attenuation

explanation: Attenuation coefficient rises with frequency; more depth → more loss.

distractorExplanations.d: Gain amplifies display, not true attenuation.

prompt: The decibel (dB) scale for intensity ratios is:

options.text: Linear always with 1 dB = 1 Pascal exactly
Logarithmic: 3 dB ≈ factor of 2; 10 dB ≈ factor of 10

explanation: Know common dB doublings for intensity/amplitude conventions taught in SPI.

options.text: The interface is large and smooth relative to wavelength
Scatterers are much smaller than wavelength only (Rayleigh)

explanation: Specular reflections are angle-dependent; diffuse scattering from rough surfaces.

options.text: Refraction—bending of the beam when speed changes across an oblique interface

explanation: Refraction can laterally displace structures (e.g., edge artifacts/duodenum-type teaching cases).

prompt: Intensity of an ultrasound beam is proportional to:

options.text: Amplitude squared (for a given medium relationship)

explanation: Acoustic intensity relates to amplitude squared (power/area considerations).

distractorExplanations.b: Incorrect: Propagation speed only, never amplitude

prompt: Refraction occurs when a sound beam:

options.text: Hits a boundary at 90° with identical impedances
Obliquely encounters a boundary between media with different propagation speeds
Is completely absorbed with no reflection ever

explanation: Snell's law: refraction needs oblique incidence and speed mismatch.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Hits a boundary at 90° with identical impedances
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Is completely absorbed with no reflection ever

options.text: The number of pulses transmitted per second
The time from the start of one pulse to the end of that pulse only

explanation: PRF is pulse rate; deeper imaging generally requires lower PRF.

distractorExplanations.b: Incorrect: The time from the start of one pulse to the end of that pulse only
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Always equal to operating frequency

prompt: Increasing imaging depth while keeping other factors constant typically requires:

options.text: Lower PRF to allow time for echoes to return

explanation: Deeper echoes take longer; PRF must decrease to avoid range ambiguity.

distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Elimination of the range equation

options.text: Relative changes in intensity or amplitude on a logarithmic scale
Only transducer footprint width in millimeters

explanation: dB express ratios (e.g., attenuation, gain changes) logarithmically.

distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Only transducer footprint width in millimeters

options.text: Certain materials convert pressure to electricity and vice versa

explanation: PZT elements transmit and receive; Curie temperature destroys piezoelectricity if overheated.

options.text: Improve transmission between PZT and tissue by intermediate impedance
Eliminate the need for gel always

explanation: Matching layer thickness ~1/4 wavelength design concept.

options.text: Increases ringing forever for better Q
Shortens spatial pulse length improving axial resolution (at cost of sensitivity)

explanation: Low-Q damped transducers for imaging; continuous wave Doppler lightly damped.

options.text: More cycles in the pulse always
Shorter spatial pulse length (higher frequency / more damping)

explanation: AR ≈ SPL/2; fewer cycles and shorter λ help.

prompt: Lateral resolution is best at the:

options.text: Extreme sides of a sector far from center always

explanation: Focusing improves lateral resolution at the focal zone; multiple foci reduce frame rate.

prompt: Linear sequenced arrays create rectangular images by:

options.text: Firing groups of elements in sequence across the face

explanation: Linear = rectangle; convex = blunted sector; phased = sector from small footprint.

options.text: Minute time delays (phasing) across elements to steer/focus the beam

explanation: Electronic beam forming enables sector imaging from small probes (cardiac).

distractorExplanations.a: Motors in some mechanical transducers historically.

options.text: Increasing output power only as the lobe fix
Apodization, subdicing, and tight element spacing concepts

explanation: Grating/side lobes create artifacts and degrade lateral resolution.

options.text: Pulse duration divided by pulse repetition period (fraction of time transmitting)

explanation: DF is unitless; CW Doppler DF = 1; imaging DF very small.

distractorExplanations.c: That's not the definition product for DF.
distractorExplanations.d: Frame rate related but not DF definition.

explanation: SPL determines axial resolution; PD = n × T.

options.text: Conversion between electrical energy and mechanical (sound) energy in crystals
Only heating of tissue without vibration

explanation: Piezoelectric materials expand/contract with voltage and generate voltage from pressure.

distractorExplanations.b: Incorrect: Only heating of tissue without vibration
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Color map assignment in Doppler

prompt: Increasing the number of transmit foci typically:

options.text: Improves lateral resolution at multiple depths but can reduce frame rate
Eliminates the need for a matching layer

explanation: Multiple foci improve lateral resolution over depth at a temporal-resolution cost.

distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Eliminates the need for a matching layer
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Removes all side lobes permanently

options.text: Number of cycles in the pulse × wavelength
Only the crystal thickness in centimeters

explanation: SPL = n × λ; shorter SPL improves axial resolution.

distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Only the crystal thickness in centimeters

prompt: A convex (curved) array produces an image that is typically:

options.text: A sector from a tiny footprint with electronic steering only like phased array
Blunted-sector shaped with a wider field at depth from sequential firing along a curved face
A perfect rectangle with no divergence

explanation: Curved arrays create blunted sector formats common in abdominal imaging.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: A sector from a tiny footprint with electronic steering only like phased array
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: A perfect rectangle with no divergence

prompt: Bandwidth of a transducer relates to:

options.text: The range of frequencies in the transmitted/received pulse

explanation: Broader bandwidth (more damping) shortens pulses and improves axial resolution.

distractorExplanations.b: Incorrect: Only the physical cable length

prompt: The Doppler shift is proportional to:

options.text: Reflector speed and the cosine of the Doppler angle, and operating frequency

explanation: fd = 2v f0 cosθ / c; angle critical for velocity estimates.

options.text: Cosine is 0 so no Doppler shift is detected

explanation: Avoid 90°; aim for ≤60° for quantitative vascular Doppler when possible.

prompt: Aliasing in pulsed Doppler occurs when:

options.text: Wall filter is appropriately set always as definition of aliasing
CW Doppler is used (CW aliases the same way)

explanation: Fix aliasing by increasing PRF/scale, adjusting baseline, lower f0, shallower sample, or switch to CW.

distractorExplanations.b: CW does not alias the same (no PRF sampling limit).

options.text: Precise depth range resolution of the source of shift
No need for two crystals/elements concepts
Ability to measure high velocities without aliasing

explanation: CW lacks range resolution; PW has sample volume depth selectivity.

prompt: Bernoulli equation simplified in echo estimates pressure gradients from:
explanation: ΔP ≈ 4v² relates peak velocity to pressure drop across stenoses/valves.

options.text: Tendency for laminar vs turbulent flow

explanation: High Reynolds (high velocity, large diameter, low viscosity) → turbulence; spectral window fill-in.

prompt: Color Doppler packet size (ensemble length) tradeoff:

options.text: Larger packets always increase frame rate
Packet size does not affect color quality
Larger packets improve velocity estimates but reduce frame rate
Packets replace ECG gating needs always

explanation: Color is multi-pulse; compromises among area, depth, packets, and PRF.

options.text: Only the highest arterial velocities always as their purpose
ECG signals in the ultrasound machine power cord
All useful diastolic venous flow by design goal when set optimally always
Low-frequency Doppler shifts from vessel wall/clutter motion

explanation: Excessive wall filter can wipe out slow flow (e.g., venous/diastolic).

options.text: Strength/amplitude of Doppler shifts rather than mean velocity/direction primarily
Exact velocity and direction like spectral with numbers always
Aliasing as its main numeric output

explanation: Power Doppler more sensitive to flow presence, less angle dependent, no direction/velocity values like color.

distractorExplanations.d: Doesn't display aliasing velocities the same.

options.text: Perfectly laminar flow with tiny sample always
Turbulent flow or large sample volume encompassing varied velocities

explanation: Fill-in of spectral window suggests turbulence or technical factors.

prompt: The Doppler equation shows Doppler shift increases when:

options.text: Reflector speed increases (toward/away component) or operating frequency increases, for a given angle factor
Propagation speed in tissue becomes infinite

explanation: fd ∝ 2v f0 cosθ / c; higher v or f0 increases shift (angle matters).

distractorExplanations.b: Incorrect: Propagation speed in tissue becomes infinite
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: The Doppler angle is exactly 90°
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: PRF is set to zero

prompt: Power Doppler imaging is particularly useful because it:

options.text: Provides exact velocity and direction as well as CW Doppler
Is more sensitive to low flow and less angle-dependent than velocity color Doppler, without showing direction/velocity magnitude the same way
Replaces spectral Doppler entirely for stenosis grading

explanation: Power Doppler encodes strength of the Doppler signal; it is relatively angle-insensitive compared with color velocity maps.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Provides exact velocity and direction as well as CW Doppler
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Replaces spectral Doppler entirely for stenosis grading

options.text: Chaotic multidirectional velocities filling the spectrum at rest
Streamlined layers with a relatively narrow range of velocities in a vessel
Only flow at 90° to the beam
Absence of any Doppler shift at all angles

explanation: Laminar flow is orderly; turbulence widens the spectral envelope.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Chaotic multidirectional velocities filling the spectrum at rest
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Only flow at 90° to the beam
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Absence of any Doppler shift at all angles

prompt: Sample volume (gate) size in pulsed Doppler affects:

options.text: Which portion of the vessel is interrogated and how much spectral broadening may appear

explanation: Larger gates include more velocity components and can increase spectral broadening.

prompt: Autocorrelation in color Doppler is used to:

options.text: Estimate mean Doppler shifts rapidly across many gates for color display
Measure only tissue harmonic imaging MI
Replace the need for a transducer

explanation: Color flow uses autocorrelation (or similar) for fast mean-velocity estimates.

distractorExplanations.b: Incorrect: Measure only tissue harmonic imaging MI
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Calibrate the thermal index printer
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Replace the need for a transducer

options.text: Highly attenuating/reflecting structures (e.g., calcification, stone, bone)
Fluid-filled simple cysts primarily as enhancement opposite
Only gain being too high as true shadow physics

explanation: Useful diagnostically for stones; distinguish from improper TGC.

prompt: Acoustic enhancement is seen distal to:
explanation: Through-transmission helps confirm cystic nature.

options.text: A mirror image only below the diaphragm always as the only form
Multiple equally spaced echoes at increasing depths from strong parallel reflectors

explanation: Comet-tail/ring-down are related variants taught in SPI.

prompt: Mirror image artifact commonly occurs around:

options.text: Strong reflectors like diaphragm/pleura creating a duplicated structure

explanation: Liver lesions can appear mirrored into lung/base regions.

prompt: Side lobe/grating lobe artifacts can create:

options.text: False echoes that appear to originate from the main beam path
Only Doppler shifts without B-mode impact

explanation: Reduce with apodization; recognize as artifact vs real masses.

options.text: Always max MI/TI for prettier images
Use output exposures As Low As Reasonably Achievable for diagnostic quality
Ultrasound is ionizing so dose like CT

explanation: Monitor MI/TI; reduce power, dwell time, and use appropriate modes.

prompt: Mechanical index (MI) relates most to risk of:

options.text: Only heating in bone exclusively as MI meaning

explanation: TI relates to heating; MI to mechanical/cavitation; contrast agents raise cavitation concern.

options.text: Exact temperature in every voxel always perfectly
Potential tissue heating from ultrasound absorption

explanation: TIS/TIB/TIC variants depend on assumptions; minimize dwell especially with bone/fetal concerns.

options.text: Echoes from deep structures return after the next pulse is sent (PRP too short)
CW Doppler is range gated precisely

explanation: Reduce by lowering PRF/scale or adjusting depth/timing expectations.

prompt: Slice thickness artifact can fill in:

options.text: Only spectral windows in a beneficial way always
Anechoic structures with false echoes from beam elevation thickness

explanation: Elevational focusing/1.5D arrays reduce slice thickness artifacts.

prompt: Comet-tail artifact is a form of:

options.text: Reverberation from closely spaced strong reflectors

explanation: Comet-tail is a dense reverberation pattern from metal/cholesterol etc.

distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Speed error from fat exclusively

prompt: Range ambiguity artifact can occur when:

options.text: PRF is so high that echoes from deep structures return after the next pulse is sent
Only CW Doppler is used for imaging depth

explanation: If listening time is too short, late echoes are mis-mapped to shallow depths.

distractorExplanations.b: Incorrect: Gain is set to zero
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Only CW Doppler is used for imaging depth
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: The matching layer is perfect

prompt: Which bioeffect is more associated with cavitation risk?

options.text: Only skin burns from the keyboard
Mechanical/nonthermal effects monitored conceptually by MI
Solely the thermal index for bone

explanation: MI relates to mechanical (cavitation) bioeffect potential.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Only skin burns from the keyboard
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Solely the thermal index for bone

options.text: Distal to the edges of a curved structure such as a cyst

explanation: Beam refraction at curved borders can create edge shadows.

distractorExplanations.b: Incorrect: Only inside the transducer housing

prompt: To practice ALARA during obstetric ultrasound, a sonographer should:

options.text: Use maximum output and dwell time on one spot indefinitely
Minimize output and exposure time while obtaining diagnostic images
Disable freeze and record for hours continuously

explanation: ALARA: as low as reasonably achievable exposure consistent with diagnostic needs.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Use maximum output and dwell time on one spot indefinitely
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Disable freeze and record for hours continuously
